versions.label: BSB
paragraphs: my heart is faint within me.
19 Listen to the cry of the daughter of my people
“Is the LORD no longer in Zion?
Is her King no longer there?”
“Why have they provoked Me to anger
20 “The harvest has passed, the summer has ended,
but we have not been saved.”
21 For the brokenness of the daughter of my people I am crushed.
I mourn; horror has gripped me.
22 Is there no balm in Gilead?
Why then has the health of the daughter of my people

context_lines: In **Jeremiah 8**, this section should be read within the flow of the chapter and the larger movement of Jeremiah.
common_interpretation_lines: A common reading of **Jeremiah Weeps for His People** is that it should be interpreted in light of the chapter's full narrative movement rather than as an isolated proof text.
Where interpreters differ on details, the immediate setting in **Jeremiah 8** remains the first control on meaning.
